agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to consider turning off LED screen at Gilmore Place
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will hear a delegation from residents Martin Kendell and Monika Curman requesting that the LED screen at Gilmore Place be turned off. The screen operates 24 hours a day and shines coloured light into neighbouring units. The residents argue the screen was approved in 2018 on the condition it would be above a commercial plaza bordered by three 50+ storey buildings (Phases 3 and 4), which the developer has paused. A petition from neighbourhood residents supports turning off the screen until Phases 3 and 4 are completed.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect signage rules for nearby developments.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
5.1
DELEGATION: Martin Kendell - Request to Turn Off LED Screen at Gilmore Place
1.
DELEGATION 430 - Martin Kendell and Monika Curman_Redacted 2026-05-04.pdf
Speakers:
Martin Kendell and Monika Curman, Residents of 4182 Dawson Street

agenda_briefs.article_title: Planning and Development Committee Meeting Agenda — May 04, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to consider easing R1 zoning rules for 33-foot lots
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will hear a delegation from Gurmit Gandhal on May 4, 2026, about challenges developing 33-foot-wide lots under the current R1 zoning bylaw. The delegation argues recent bylaw changes limit design flexibility and reduce feasibility for standard 33-foot lots. No decision has been made — council will consider whether to update the bylaw to allow more flexibility for narrower lots.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if the bylaw changes, it could affect your development plans for narrow lots.

key_dates.label: Delegation hearing — Burnaby Planning and Development Committee

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 32-storey and 28-storey apartments at Silver Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is considering rezoning (REZ #25-12) for two apartment towers at 6470 and 6508 Silver Avenue: a 32-storey south tower and a 28-storey north tower, totaling 784 units (including 83 rental replacement units). The proposal includes 561 vehicle parking spaces (0.72/unit), 1,637 secured bicycle spaces, and a 6-metre pedestrian linkage to Maywood Park. Council will vote on advancing to first and second reading; a public hearing would follow if approved.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed rezoning.

key_dates.label: PDC meeting to consider advancing rezoning to first reading

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Mixed-use high-rise rezoning on Dow Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is considering forwarding REZ #25-23 to First and Second Reading for a mixed-use high-rise development at 6645–6707 Dow Avenue. The proposal includes two towers (45 and 35 storeys) and a 6-storey building, with 1,065 dwelling units (including 112 rental replacements), 1,510 m² of commercial space (703.1 m² office, 806.9 m² retail), and 888 vehicle parking spaces. The project requires a Housing Agreement under the Rental Use Zoning Policy – Stream 1, with non-market rental units restricted from stratification and rented at eligible rates. Prerequisites include road dedications (1.8 m on Dow, 10.06 m on Beresford, 6.1 m lane), a public art plan or cash-in-lieu, and a Tenant Assistance Plan.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on the proposed rezoning.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2050 OCP Special Study Area program framework
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a proposed program framework for Special Study Areas in the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP). The OCP is the city's long-term plan for growth and land use. Special Study Areas are specific locations where more detailed planning is needed before any new development rules are set. This framework would outline how the city will study these areas and decide on future land use changes. A staff presentation on the proposal is scheduled for the next council meeting. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision and any future public consultation opportunities — this framework could affect development rules in your area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
VERBAL REPORT: BURNABY 2050 OCP SPECIAL STUDY AREA PROGRAM FRAMEWORK
Purpose: To provide information on a proposed program framework to address Special Study Areas in the Burnaby 2050 OCP.
Staff presentation by Hay Go, Planner 2
